tasks.title: Fail fast on malformed README version markers (don't default to version 1)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/init/readme.rs:48-60: extract_readme_version returns None for malformed marker values.
crates/ralph/src/commands/init/readme.rs:85 and :116: call sites use `.unwrap_or(1)`, treating malformed markers as legacy v1 silently.
Failure mode: README update logic can misclassify and hide a corrupted version marker.
plan: Change `extract_readme_version` to return a richer type (e.g., `Result<u32>` or `(Option<u32>, bool marker_present)`) so callers can distinguish legacy/no-marker from malformed marker.
Update `check_readme_current_from_root` / `write_readme` to: treat 'no marker' as legacy v1; treat 'marker present but invalid' as `Err` with an actionable message.
Add tests: invalid marker yields `Err`; no marker yields v1; valid marker parses correctly.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Preserve timeout diagnostics when output-capture mutex is poisoned
evidence: crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs:198-205: wrap_output_handler_with_capture drops capture writes if mutex lock fails (including PoisonError).
crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs:299-311: timeout path reads capture via `capture.lock().map(...).unwrap_or_default()`, discarding PoisonError and losing captured output.
Operability impact: timeouts can lose diagnostic output, undermining safeguard dumps.
plan: Handle PoisonError explicitly with `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())` when locking capture for both writing and reading; log a warning when poison is detected.
Add a unit test that poisons the mutex (panic while holding lock), then invokes the handler and asserts capture still records output and does not panic.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Deduplicate TaskEditKey field formatting to prevent TUI/CLI drift
evidence: crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs:630-658: TaskEditKey -> value mapping for preview formatting.
DRY risk: overlapping keys (Request/Duplicates/*At fields) are implemented twice and can drift.
plan: Introduce a shared helper for TaskEditKey field extraction/formatting (in queue module or on Task) and use it from both the TUI editor and the preview code.
Update both call sites to use the shared helper.
Add tests covering a representative Task to ensure both paths render the same values for shared keys.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Split oversized TUI app module (3347 LOC) into cohesive submodules
evidence: AGENTS.md guideline: files >1000 LOC should be split; >800 LOC requires explicit justification.
plan: Identify cohesive responsibilities inside app.rs (state, actions, event handling, IO, rendering adapters).
Extract into new internal modules under crates/ralph/src/tui/ (e.g., app_state.rs, app_actions.rs, app_events.rs), keeping public surface minimal.
Keep behavior stable by wiring through existing entrypoints and updating imports.
Add/adjust tests for extracted modules where behavior is tricky or under-tested.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Split oversized parallel run module (3241 LOC) into cohesive submodules
evidence: AGENTS.md guideline: files >1000 LOC should be split; >800 LOC requires explicit justification.
plan: Map responsibilities in parallel/mod.rs (scheduling, worker lifecycle, PR operations, merge policy, state persistence).
Extract cohesive internal modules (e.g., scheduler.rs, worker.rs, pr_ops.rs, state_io.rs) under crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/.
Keep behavior stable by leaving public APIs in mod.rs and delegating to submodules.
Add/adjust tests covering critical paths (state file reading/writing, PR record reconciliation, merge policy decisions).
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Warn on .env load errors (ignore NotFound only)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/main.rs:31: `dotenvy::dotenv().ok();` ignores parse/IO errors when a .env is present.
Repro: create an invalid .env in a temp dir and run `ralph --help` from that dir; current code emits no warning, making misconfiguration hard to diagnose.
plan: Change dotenv loading to ignore only NotFound; capture other errors and surface them as a warning (e.g., log after logger init, or print a single redacted warning to stderr).
Add an integration test that runs the ralph binary from a temp dir containing an invalid .env and asserts stderr contains a warning while exit code remains success.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
